response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with a genuine craftsmanship ethos, but he is a poor match for this specific role. The position requires 8 years of C++ and 5 years of large-scale infrastructure experience; his C++ exposure amounts to roughly a year (National Instruments plus a thesis project), his systems-level work is primarily in DLang over about two years, and his career centers on web and product development rather than cloud compute platforms. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, making the seniority and domain gaps too large for a Technical Lead on a cloud compute platform.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests per second)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement, with only about one year of professional C/C++ work
Limited large-scale distributed-infrastructure and cloud-architecture depth; most recent work is product-focused TypeScript/Node.js
No formal technical-leadership experience leading platform strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineering teams

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud infrastructure and distributed-systems depth
